text: Finally something I actually understand. These big companies betting on nuclear makes me want to add some CEG to the college fund.

text: different layers of the same process. ASML does the printing, AMAT does the deposition and etching before and after. both have monopolies in their own lanes.

author: hype_man

text: memory cycle thesis is solid. MU has the best HBM3E in the industry and the forward multiple is still reasonable. could work out.
author: quiet_observer

text: bought GOOGL and it dropped immediately of course. but waymo seems like the real deal and cloud business is growing. just gonna sit on it and stop checking
author: bought_at_the_top

text: alphabet is the most underappreciated mega cap out there. waymo alone could be worth half a trillion in a few years and people act like its nothing. long term hold.

author: crypto_refugee_88

text: waymo is the real hidden gem in GOOGL. market is pricing alphabet like a dying ad company but cloud and autonomous driving are the growth engines nobody credits them for.

text: valuation is fair but the ad revenue concern is real. waymo is promising but not profitable yet. worth keeping an eye on.
author: market_debater

text: bought SPCX on IPO day like a lemming. down a bit but musk is crazy enough to make this work long term. should i buy more or is that stupid
author: help_me_pls77

text: im not checking my SPCX position til next year. space is a long game and musk has a track record of delivering even when he looks crazy. set it and forget it.

author: mom_of_3_trading

text: everyone is missing the macro angle on IREN. stranded power assets are becoming incredibly valuable as AI data center buildout accelerates. the bitcoin mining infrastructure pivot to AI is one of the most creative capital allocation stories this year.

text: crowdstrike is the gold standard for endpoint security. my company switched to falcon last year and the difference is night and day. subscription revenue model is a cash machine.

author: bought_at_the_top

text: fintech is eating banking and SOFI has the best execution in the space. the bank charter gives them a real funding advantage now. multi-year compounder here.
author: CCWaterBug

text: member growth at this scale with improving unit economics is the goldilocks scenario. most fintechs grow or profit but rarely both. the trajectory is what matters.
author: thesis_driven_dan

text: sofi is winning actual banking customers not just fintech tourists. bank charter was the unlock. the market will realize it eventually.
author: hype_man

text: growth story is compelling but the galileo overhang is real. need to see that business segment stabilize before the multiple re-rates.
author: market_debater

text: 35 billion in debt sounds insane but everyone keeps saying ai compute demand is bottomless. is this basically a leveraged bet on the whole ai buildout?
author: help_me_pls77

text: debt heavy and widely hated. the classic contrarian setup. if ai demand keeps compounding the balance sheet becomes an afterthought. if it slows though the pain is real.
author: whohebe123

text: the bull case rests on gpu compute being structurally undersupplied through 2027 and coreweave having locked in long term contracts. the bear case is equally simple: demand softens and the leverage becomes existential.
author: Latrodectus1990

text: debt is a tool not a death sentence if your revenue growth outpaces the interest. coreweaves revenue is scaling fast enough for now.
author: quiet_observer

text: so crowdstrike actually bounced back from that outage thing?? i dont even know what they do but my friend keeps bringing up this ticker
author: first_time_caller99

text: they basically lost their biggest enterprise customers overnight but the stock recovered like nothing happened. demand for cybersecurity is just that sticky
author: CarrotAwesome

text: been burned by security stocks before. the recovery looks legit but im not jumping in till it proves itself again
author: still_holding_2021

text: i was confused too at first. they make endpoint protection software basically every big company uses it to stop hackers. kinda runs behind the scenes of corporate networks
author: quiet_observer

text: thats what i keep saying. switching costs in cyber are insane. once youre integrated you dont just rip it out. the moat is basically lock-in + genuine need
author: market_debater

text: quantum computing feels like crypto in the early days. im trying to figure out if this is gonna explode or be a grind that lasts forever
author: crypto_refugee_88

text: my husband works in tech and keeps telling me quantum is the next massive thing. just not sure when that future actually gets here for investors
author: mom_of_3_trading

text: ive watched quantum stocks get pumped on promises every few years. the hardware keeps improving but commercial usefulness keeps moving out. classic story in this space

text: the hardware improvements are accelerating faster than most people think. last year nobody expected qubit counts this high this soon. the graph is going up way faster than before

text: everyone freaking out about the debt is missing the point. when every hyperscaler cant build gpu capacity fast enough and you have contracted demand this heavy the leverage works for you not against you
